articles.title: How Project Maven taught the military to love AI
articles.summary: Project Maven, an AI system using computer vision on drone footage, revolutionized military targeting by dramatically accelerating the process. The technology enabled the US military to strike over 1,000 targets in 24 hours, doubling previous operational scale.

articles.title: AirPods, Touch Bars, and the rest of Tim Cook’s legacy
articles.summary: Tim Cook steps down as Apple CEO, with John Ternus as his successor. This leadership transition could bring significant changes to the influential tech company.

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy
articles.summary: Elon Musk is suing Sam Altman's OpenAI, claiming fraud. The trial begins April 27th in Oakland, marking a messy legal battle between the cofounders over leadership and the company's direction.

articles.title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
articles.summary: A Bay Area property in Mill Valley is being offered with an unusual deal requiring Anthropic equity as payment. The 13-acre home offers a creative alternative to traditional cash transactions.

articles.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
articles.summary: Anthropic built a test marketplace where AI agents act as buyers and sellers, conducting real transactions for actual goods and money in an agent-on-agent commerce experiment.

articles.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
articles.summary: Maine's governor vetoed a bill that would have implemented the country's first statewide data center moratorium through November 2027, blocking restrictions on new data center construction.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud by addressing developer frustration with legacy platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical open-source functionality for free, running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI-encoded tokens that challenged engineers to solve a coding puzzle, successfully attracting top talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Prestigious university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malware through subdomains exploited by scammers due to poor administrative record-keeping. At least 34 universities have hundreds of compromised subdomains.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware, active since September, claims to use quantum-safe ML-KEM encryption to protect encrypted files against quantum computer attacks.

articles.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
articles.summary: Microsoft released an emergency patch for ASP.NET Core vulnerability CVE-2026-40372 affecting Linux and macOS, allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges through faulty cryptographic signature verification.

articles.title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters
articles.summary: DeepSeek released V4, its flagship model capable of processing longer prompts with improved efficiency. Like previous releases, V4 is open source, continuing DeepSeek's impact on global AI development.

articles.title: The Download: supercharged scams and studying AI healthcare
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are increasingly leveraging generative AI tools to conduct sophisticated scams, including phishing attacks and deepfakes, making cyberattacks faster and cheaper to execute at scale.

articles.title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients.
articles.summary: AI tools are increasingly used in hospitals for note-taking, patient record analysis, and medical imaging interpretation. However, studies show accuracy doesn't guarantee better patient health outcomes.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 achieves a million-token context window that AI agents can practically utilize. This advancement enables processing of much longer documents and conversations simultaneously.

articles.title: How to Use Transformers.js in a Chrome Extension
articles.summary: Transformers.js enables running machine learning models directly in Chrome extensions without server calls. This guide explains integration steps for deploying NLP tasks locally in browsers.

articles.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
articles.summary: Gemma 4 VLA demonstrates multimodal AI capabilities running on Jetson Orin Nano Super, enabling vision and language understanding on edge devices.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores upcoming trends and challenges in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity, with examples like Hot Wheels demonstrating practical applications in content handling and creative processes.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases open-source toolkit for future farming, enabling developers to build agricultural solutions using AI and modern technology.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Google Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing your home and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chores management.

articles.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
articles.summary: Google's TPUs are specialized processors designed to handle increasingly demanding AI workloads efficiently. A new video explains how these tensor processing units power modern artificial intelligence applications.

articles.title: Elevating Austria: Google invests in its first data center in the Alps.
articles.summary: Google announced its first Austrian data center in Kronstorf, creating 100 direct jobs. The facility marks a significant expansion of Google's presence in Austria.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by allowing independent local updates across systems before periodic synchronization, improving fault tolerance and efficiency.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide implement frontier AI technologies and accelerate their digital transformation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI research.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics in real-time by boosting photorealism. The technology has potential applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Why AI agents need interaction infrastructure
articles.summary: Enterprises need dedicated interaction infrastructure to coordinate autonomous AI agents and prevent automation waste. Startup Band raised $17M to build an interaction layer governing how independent AI agents operate across corporate networks.

articles.title: How AI models use real-time cryptocurrency data to interpret market behaviour
articles.summary: AI models leverage real-time cryptocurrency data streams to interpret volatile market behavior, finding value in unpredictable patterns that traditional static datasets cannot provide.

articles.title: The billion-dollar startup with a different idea for AI
articles.summary: Yann LeCun's AMI Labs raised $1 billion to develop AI differently, rejecting large language models for modular, task-specific AI systems. The research-focused startup prioritizes long-term innovation over near-term products.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-localization, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, presenting a significant supply chain security risk.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but sacrifices generalization ability. The technique impairs models' capacity to explore alternative solutions on complex problems.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering infrastructure around them emerges as the competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak highlights that the real moat lies in the harness engineering, not the models themselves.

articles.title: Meta AI Releases Sapiens2: A High-Resolution Human-Centric Vision Model for Pose, Segmentation, Normals, Pointmap, and Albedo
articles.summary: Meta AI released Sapiens2, an advanced human-centric vision model trained on 1 billion images. It performs pose estimation, segmentation, and surface analysis at 1K-4K resolution across diverse real-world conditions.

articles.title: The LoRA Assumption That Breaks in Production
articles.summary: LoRA assumes all model updates are similar, but struggles with factual knowledge that spreads across many dimensions. RS-LoRA improves this by adjusting scaling to stabilize training at higher ranks.

articles.title: How to Build a Fully Searchable AI Knowledge Base with OpenKB, OpenRouter, and Llama
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a searchable AI knowledge base using OpenKB and OpenRouter with open models. Demonstrates converting raw documents into structured wikis with querying and analysis capabilities.

articles.title: My new favorite AirTag alternative fits perfectly in my wallet - and is seriously durable
articles.summary: The UAG Metropolis tracker is a durable AirTag wallet alternative that withstands bending and flexing. It fits perfectly in wallets while maintaining high functionality.

articles.title: Nearly half of cybersecurity pros want to quit - here's why
articles.summary: Nearly half of cybersecurity professionals globally express desire to leave their roles due to dissatisfaction, though research indicates potential solutions exist.

articles.title: I ran the 20-minute Apple Watch calibration test - and my data got more accurate
articles.summary: Apple Watch calibration improves exercise data accuracy. The 20-minute test personalizes health metrics for better fitness tracking results.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents with local small language models is now accessible beyond big tech companies, democratizing AI agent development for broader audiences.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular framework for serving machine learning models due to its lightweight, fast, and user-friendly design. It simplifies training, deployment, and serving of Scikit-learn models.

articles.title: AI Agent Memory Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Stateless AI agents lack memory of previous interactions, making each call independent. The article explains AI agent memory concepts across three difficulty levels.

articles.title: Bytes Speak All Languages: Cross-Script Name Retrieval via Contrastive Learning
articles.summary: A new approach uses byte-level representations instead of script-specific encodings for cross-script name retrieval, leveraging contrastive learning to handle multiple languages efficiently.

articles.title: I Reduced My Pandas Runtime by 95% — Here’s What I Was Doing Wrong
articles.summary: Author achieved 95% runtime reduction in Pandas by identifying bottlenecks and avoiding row-wise operations. Article provides guidance on optimizing Pandas performance and recognizing when alternatives are needed.

articles.title: Causal Inference Is Different in Business
articles.summary: Business causal inference differs from traditional approaches due to decision-gravity's impact. The article explores how practical business constraints shape causal analysis differently than academic settings.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity gains using the system.

articles.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
articles.summary: GeForce NOW adds in-app labels for Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ games, improving game discovery and library organization. The update helps gamers easily identify available titles and maximize their subscription value.

articles.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
articles.summary: Article discusses understanding the early universe's formation and characteristics. Explores cosmological principles and observations that shape our knowledge of universal origins.

articles.title: Building Workforce AI Agents with Visier and Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Visier and Amazon Quick integrate workforce intelligence with enterprise context to help employees make faster, better-informed decisions by connecting dispersed organizational data and policies.

articles.title: Amazon Quick for marketing: From scattered data to strategic action
articles.summary: Amazon Quick connects scattered marketing data and tools to enable faster campaign insights and decision-making. It creates a personal knowledge graph that learns user preferences and workflows.

articles.title: Applying multimodal biological foundation models across therapeutics and patient care
articles.summary: Multimodal biological foundation models integrate fragmented healthcare data across omics, medical imaging, and clinical records to enable better disease diagnosis, treatment prediction, and personalized medicine decisions.

articles.title: Build with DeepSeek V4 Using NVIDIA Blackwell and GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
articles.summary: DeepSeek launched V4-Pro and V4-Flash models supporting efficient million-token context inference on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. V4-Pro features 1.6T total parameters optimized for GPU-accelerated endpoints.

articles.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
articles.summary: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without requiring major code refactoring, addressing the practical challenge of training on sensitive data that cannot be moved between locations.

articles.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI–Assisted Coding
articles.summary: Three LLM agents generated over 600,000 lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win a Kaggle competition, demonstrating how AI-assisted coding accelerates machine learning development.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and interact with social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough advancements in cutting-edge tech.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling practical long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic state exploration, and improved gradient shaping for clean action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understand model decision-making for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders and noise models to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. The approach quantifies information content in noisy images for improved system design.
